meta.tagline: Day 22. Israel hit Tehran again overnight. US bunker-busted Natanz. Trump says winding down — strikes say otherwise. Oil above $110. S&P down 1.5%. Iran threatened the UAE. Saturday.
meta.greeting: Good morning, Café crew — it's Saturday and the war entered week four.

headlines: 💣 Israel striking Tehran again — US bunker-busted Natanz nuclear facility
📉 S&P 500 down 1.51% — Dow off 444 points, VIX spiked 11%
🛢️ Oil up 45% since war began — Strait of Hormuz near-total shutdown
🇮🇷 Iran threatens to hit UAE after attacks from its territory
⚖️ Elon Musk found liable — jury says he cheated Twitter investors
📻 CBS Radio shut down after 99 years — part of mass layoffs

articles.title: US Dropped Bunker Busters on Iran's Nuclear Facility. Again.
articles.excerpt: The US used GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrator bombs on Natanz this morning. Israel struck Tehran again overnight. Day 22. Strikes are intensifying, not winding down.
articles.body:
The US dropped bunker-buster bombs on Iran's Natanz uranium-enrichment facility this morning. These aren't small bombs. The GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrator weighs 30,000 pounds and is designed to destroy underground targets.

Israel's defense minister said strikes will 'significantly escalate' this week. Israel hit Tehran again overnight. Iranian attacks are down 90% from early days, per the Pentagon.

Meanwhile, Trump posted on Truth Social that the US is 'getting very close to meeting our objectives.' The Pentagon is using 30,000-pound bombs. Those are not winding-down bombs.

Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei remains unseen. Intelligence agencies can't confirm he's giving orders. A US official called it 'beyond weird.' Day 22 of a war that was supposed to be quick.
articles.barista_take: Trump says winding down. Pentagon dropped 30,000-pound bombs on a nuclear facility. Words and bombs sending different messages. ☕

articles.title: Iran Just Threatened to Bomb the UAE
articles.excerpt: Iran warned the UAE: any more attacks from your territory and we'll hit Ras Al Khaimah with 'heavy strikes.' The war is pulling in countries that aren't in it.
articles.body:
Iran's military warned the UAE: if any further aggression comes from UAE territory against Iranian islands in the Gulf, Iran will subject Ras Al Khaimah to 'heavy strikes.'

The UAE isn't at war with Iran. But attacks on disputed islands apparently came from UAE territory. The UAE arrested five members of an alleged Iran-Hezbollah terror network.

Saudi Arabia intercepted 10 drones in the east. Kuwait's air defenses were responding to missiles. The UK warned Iran against targeting British bases.

The war started between the US, Israel, and Iran. It's now threatening the entire Gulf. 'Contained' has left the building.
articles.barista_take: Iran threatened the UAE. Saudi shooting down drones. Kuwait under missiles. 'Contained' has left the chat. ☕

articles.title: Cluster Munitions Hit a Daycare. Israel Cancelled Passover Camps.
articles.excerpt: An Iranian cluster munition struck a daycare in Rishon Lezion. Passover camps cancelled in 18 municipalities. The war reached playgrounds.
articles.body:
A cluster munition from an Iranian ballistic missile struck a daycare in Rishon Lezion this morning. Israel cancelled Passover holiday camps in 18 municipalities.

Cluster munitions scatter smaller explosives over a wide area. One landed on a daycare. The building was damaged by shrapnel.

Israel's defense minister said strikes on Iran will 'significantly escalate' this week. The cycle of escalation found a new floor — and it's a daycare.

Day 22. Both sides call this a war of survival. Children's camps are being cancelled.
articles.barista_take: Cluster munitions on a daycare. Passover camps cancelled. The war isn't somewhere else anymore. ☕

articles.title: CBS Radio Died After 99 Years. Nobody Had a Good Day.
articles.excerpt: CBS shut down its radio news — running since 1927, before CBS itself existed. 99 years of broadcast journalism, gone in a layoff memo. Same week the First Amendment got stress-tested.
articles.body:
CBS shut down its radio news service this week. It ran since September 1927 — before the CBS network existed. For 99 years, CBS Radio delivered news to 700 stations.

Gone. Part of a round of layoffs at CBS News.

Same week a judge ruled Pentagon press restrictions unconstitutional. Same week the FCC threatened broadcast licenses. American journalism is having a week that future professors will teach entire courses about.

The medium that carried Edward R. Murrow during WWII couldn't survive corporate layoffs during a new war.
articles.barista_take: 99 years of CBS Radio. Gone in a memo. Murrow is rolling. ☕

articles.title: S&P Down 1.5%. Dow Off 444. VIX Spiked 11%. The Numbers.
articles.excerpt: S&P at 6,506. Dow off 444 points. Nasdaq fell 2%. VIX fear index spiked 11%. HSBC says markets are pricing in recession. Oil is the problem nobody can solve.
articles.body:
Friday's close: S&P 500 at 6,506 — down 1.51%. Dow at 45,577 — down 444 points. Nasdaq at 21,648 — down 2.01%. VIX spiked 11.3% to 26.78.

HSBC says markets are 'pricing in a recessionary outcome.' The Strait of Hormuz is in near-total shutdown. Oil has risen 45% since the war started.

The US lifted sanctions on Iranian oil on ships as a 30-day measure. Economist EJ Antoni: 'I don't think this economy can handle $100 oil.'

It's at $110. And climbing.
articles.barista_take: S&P down 1.5%. Dow off 444. VIX up 11%. The market's horoscope: absolutely not. ☕

articles.title: Gold Crashed 3% on Friday. During a War. Here's Why.
articles.excerpt: Gold dropped from $4,700 to $4,575 — during a war, with VIX spiking. That's backwards. The answer: margin calls. Funds sold gold to cover stock losses.
articles.body:
Gold dropped 3.1% Friday to $4,575. During a war. When VIX spiked 11%. That's backwards.

When stocks crash hard, leveraged funds sell whatever has gains to cover losses. Gold was up big this year. It got sold to pay for stock losses.

This is the 'sell everything' phase. It happened in March 2020 too — gold crashed alongside stocks before reversing hard.

The fundamentals — war, inflation, uncertainty — haven't changed. The price just got cheaper for a technical reason. If you were waiting for a gold entry, this might be it.
articles.barista_take: Gold down 3% during a war. Not because gold is broken. Because everything else is. ☕

articles.title: Gas Heading to $5. Oil Up 45% in 22 Days.
articles.excerpt: Hormuz is shut. Oil up 45% since Feb 28. Gas heading to $5 nationally. Zimbabwe hit $2/liter. Fill up this weekend before $110 oil reaches your pump.
articles.body:
Oil has risen 45% since the war began. The Strait of Hormuz — 20% of the world's oil — is in near-total shutdown.

Gas heading to $5/gallon in the US. Zimbabwe hit $2/liter. The EU is calling for a moratorium on strikes against energy facilities.

The US lifted sanctions on Iranian oil on ships — a 30-day band-aid until April 19. Not enough.

Fill up this weekend. $110 oil hasn't fully hit your pump yet. It will by Wednesday.
articles.barista_take: Oil up 45% in 22 days. Hormuz shut. Gas heading to $5. Fill up today. ☕

articles.title: Jury: Musk Deliberately Tanked Twitter's Stock to Buy It Cheap
articles.excerpt: Musk didn't disclose his 5% stake on time, kept buying cheap, saved $150M. The jury called it securities fraud. Irony won this round.
articles.body:
A jury found Musk liable for deliberately driving down Twitter's stock before his $44B acquisition. He didn't disclose his 5% ownership on time, kept buying at lower prices, and saved $150M.

The jury called it securities fraud. They did absolve him of some other allegations, but the core finding stands.

The man who bought Twitter to 'save free speech' got found liable for not speaking up when the law required it.

Damages hearing to follow.
articles.barista_take: Musk saved $150M by staying quiet. The jury said that's fraud. Free speech has a disclosure deadline. ☕

articles.title: Bitcoin Is Quietly Winning the War Trade
articles.excerpt: Bitcoin gained 1.3% Friday while stocks crashed and gold got sold. Since the war started, it's outperformed S&P, Nasdaq, and gold. CNBC called it the 'surprise war hedge.'
articles.body:
While stocks crashed and gold got margin-called Friday, Bitcoin gained 1.3% to $70,947.

Since the war began, Bitcoin has outperformed the S&P 500, Nasdaq, and gold. CNBC called it the 'surprise war hedge.'

The thesis: Bitcoin isn't tied to oil supply chains, can't be sanctioned, and doesn't need Hormuz. In a world where physical commodities are weaponized, digital assets are suddenly interesting.

Polymarket gives gold 47% chance of being 2026's best asset, Bitcoin 39%, S&P just 14%.
articles.barista_take: Stocks crashed. Gold got margin-called. Bitcoin went up. 2026 is weird. ☕

articles.title: The First Amendment Had the Busiest Week of Its Life
articles.excerpt: FCC threatened licenses. A judge tossed Pentagon press rules. CBS Radio died. Three punches to journalism in one week.
articles.body:
Three things happened to journalism this week:

The FCC threatened to revoke broadcast licenses for 'fake news' about the war. A Republican senator from his own party pushed back.

A federal judge tossed Pentagon press restrictions as unconstitutional. The NYT brought the lawsuit.

CBS shut down its 99-year-old radio news service in a layoff round.

One branch tried to silence press. Another said that's unconstitutional. And a 99-year institution died in a memo. The First Amendment got a stress test.
articles.barista_take: FCC threatens press. Judge defends press. CBS fires press. The First Amendment needs a drink. ☕

articles.title: Three Careers This War Just Invented
articles.excerpt: Compliance analysts. Geopolitical risk modelers. Cleared cyber. Three careers that barely existed before Feb 28. All hiring now.
articles.body:
Every crisis creates careers:

Securities compliance analyst — the Musk verdict reminded every company disclosure timing matters. No law degree needed. Just detail and spreadsheets.

Geopolitical risk analyst — oil moved 45% in 22 days because of a strait most people can't find on a map. Every fund needs someone who can model this. Six figures.

Cleared cybersecurity — the cyber war is massive. Get a clearance and Palantir, Booz Allen, Leidos will fight over you.
articles.barista_take: Compliance. Geopolitical risk. Cleared cyber. Three careers the last 22 days invented. ☕

articles.title: The Saturday LinkedIn Move Nobody's Making
articles.excerpt: The algorithm doesn't take weekends off. Your competition does. One post about today's news. 20 minutes. Zero competition in the feed.
articles.body:
Free career move. 20 minutes. Post on LinkedIn on Saturday.

The algorithm doesn't sleep. Your competition does. Saturday posts get dramatically less competition.

Today's pitch: 'The US just bunker-busted Iran's nuclear facility. Here's what it means in 60 seconds.' 300 words. Tag three people. Post.

Do this every Saturday for a month. Recruiters scroll on weekends too. Scale to a paid Substack at $7/month. 1,000 subs = $7K/month.
articles.barista_take: Saturday. LinkedIn. 20 minutes. Zero competition. Your future boss scrolls on weekends. ☕

articles.title: Week 4 of War News. Your Brain Is Running on Fumes.
articles.excerpt: 22 days of cortisol. Your prefrontal cortex is degraded. One hour offline isn't lazy — it's neuroscience.
articles.body:
22 straight days of war news creates measurable cognitive fatigue. Slower decisions. Lower motivation. Less creativity.

Your prefrontal cortex processes information worse after three weeks of cortisol. This isn't weakness. It's biology.

One hour today: no news, no markets, no war. Your brain needs the break to keep caring well. The war will be there at noon.
articles.barista_take: Day 22. Cortisol maxed. One hour offline. Neuroscience says so. ☕

articles.title: $110 Oil Is Ruining Your Sleep
articles.excerpt: Financial stress from gas and groceries is the #1 sleep disruptor in 2026. A 10-minute notebook brain dump cuts sleep onset by 15 minutes. Free.
articles.body:
Financial stress from rising gas and grocery prices is now the #1 reported sleep disruptor in the US.

The fix: 10-minute pre-sleep brain dump. Write every financial worry on paper. Not to solve them. To get them out of your head.

Reduces sleep onset time by 15 minutes on average. Free sleeping pill courtesy of a $2 notebook.
articles.barista_take: $110 oil keeping you up. A notebook and 10 minutes. Cheaper than melatonin. ☕

articles.title: Three Wins. One Priority. Before the News.
articles.excerpt: The whole system fits on a Post-it. Three completions. One Monday priority. Written before you open a single app.
articles.body:
Four minutes. Before news or social media:

Write three things you completed this week. Not planned — completed. Most people undercount wins by 40%.

Pick one thing for Monday. Not most urgent — most important. Urgent screams. Important whispers.

Three wins. One priority. Post-it. Before the news. That's it.
articles.barista_take: Three wins. One Monday priority. Post-it. Before Twitter. That's literally it. ☕

articles.title: 47 Tabs Open. Your Brain Has Room for One.
articles.excerpt: Close all tabs except one. 25-minute timer. Then the next. Your brain has 2GB of RAM and you're running 47 apps.
articles.body:
47 tabs open. Your fan is the evidence.

One-tab rule: close everything except the current task. 25-minute timer. Done. Next tab.

Every open tab is a cognitive thread running in the background. 47 threads on 2GB of RAM.

'But I need those tabs later.' Bookmark them. They'll exist Monday. Your focus won't.
articles.barista_take: 47 tabs. Zero focus. Close 46. Timer. Your RAM called — it wants its life back. ☕

articles.title: Turn Saturday's News Into Side Income
articles.excerpt: Bunker busters. Market crashes. Nobody has time for 15 articles. You explain it in 300 words. 45 minutes. $7K/month at scale.
articles.body:
Every Saturday the news explodes. Most people want it explained but don't have 3 hours.

Step 1: Pick today's biggest story. 300 words. Plain English.
Step 2: Post on LinkedIn. Punchy opener. Tag three people.
Step 3: Every Saturday for a month. 1,000 followers → paid Substack at $7/month.

1,000 subs × $7 = $7,000/month. For 45 minutes on Saturdays. The news is the content. You're the translator.
articles.barista_take: The news is the content. You're the translator. 45 minutes. $7K/month at scale. ☕

articles.title: "You Can't Calm the Storm. Calm Yourself."
articles.excerpt: Bunker busters on nuclear facilities. Cluster munitions on daycares. Markets crashing. None of it is in your control. Your first hour is.
articles.body:
"You can't calm the storm. So stop trying. What you can do is calm yourself." — Timber Hawkeye

Bunker busters on Natanz. Cluster munitions on daycares. Markets pricing recession. Gold crashing. Oil at $110. CBS Radio dead.

None of that is in your control.

Your first hour today is. The war will be there at noon. Use the morning.
articles.quote: You can't calm the storm. So stop trying. What you can do is calm yourself.
articles.barista_take: 22 days of storm. Your first hour is yours. Use it before the news takes it. ☕

on_this_day.text: US invaded Iraq — Operation Iraqi Freedom. 23 years later, US forces are bunker-busting another country's nuclear facilities. History plagiarizes aggressively.
CBS Radio launched its first broadcast. This week, 99 years later, CBS shut it down. Murrow's medium didn't survive a corporate memo.
John Lennon married Yoko Ono in Gibraltar. They held a Bed-In for Peace. Significantly fewer bunker busters.

meme.closer: It's Saturday. The news didn't get the memo. ☕

cafe_wins: ✅ One hour offline — no news, no markets, no war updates
📋 Write 3 things you accomplished this week
⛽ Fill up your gas tank — oil at $110 and climbing
📋 Write your Monday priority before opening Slack
☕ One full hour of focused work before lunch
